explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 57 from a base nutrition score of 53. Context lifted it by 4 points.

topPros.title: a true outlier for fruit fat quality
topPros.explanation: This behaves nothing like typical sweet fruit and that makes the category result exceptional.

topPros.title: easy to use as a whole food
topPros.explanation: Simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating.

topPros.title: brings a clean fruit identity
topPros.explanation: It still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

topCons.title: calorie density is much higher than most fruits
topCons.explanation: Still useful, but it changes how casually you can throw it in.

topCons.title: sugar can climb fast
topCons.explanation: A lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet.

topCons.title: satiety is not especially strong
topCons.explanation: Fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 4 points.

closing.summary: Avocado has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.

Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
